qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the reduction in capacity of the Niagara and Mist clusters starting January 6, 2025, and when were they fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning January 6, 2025, the Niagara and Mist clusters operated at reduced capacities (approximately 50% and 35% respectively) to support ongoing system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system. Mist was fully decommissioned on September 16, 2025, and Niagara was fully decommissioned on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: How did the Graham compute cluster operate starting February 25, 2025, and when was it fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning February 25, 2025, the Graham compute cluster operated at approximately 25% capacity while data migration and system transition work were underway ahead of its retirement. Graham was fully decommissioned on September 1, 2025, with Graham Cloud remaining fully operational throughout this period.

qa_pairs.question: What services were unavailable for Béluga, Narval, and Juno clusters between July 15 and September 8, 2025, due to the tape storage system migration?
qa_pairs.answer: From July 15 to August 25, 2025, the tape storage system behind the TSM service, including backups and migrated /nearline data, was unavailable. This meant file backup and restore services were unavailable, data created or modified between July 15 and August 11 could not be recovered, and files in /nearline that had been migrated to tape on Béluga and Narval were inaccessible. The TSM service itself was fully unavailable.
